items.title: Three firefighters die battling huge wildfires on Colorado-Utah border
items.description: A number of blazes have been raging across Utah and Colorado, leading both governors to declare states of emergency.

items.title: Trade tensions shake up Brazil's caipirinha spirit
items.description: U.S. tariff pressure is pushing Europe and Brazil closer—opening new global doors for everything from aircraft parts to Brazil's cachaça, the base of the caipirinha.

items.title: The week in pictures: Earthquakes in Venezuela, Starmer's resignation and extreme heat in France
items.description: The resignation of British Prime Minister Keir Starmer less than two years after his election victory, extreme heat grips France, fans fete the World Cup and twin earthquakes ravage Venezuela: FRANCE 24 looks back at some of the week's most striking images.

items.title: Germany news: New heat record at 41.7 degrees Celsius
items.description: For the third consecutive day, the DWD German Weather Service has reported a record temperature. The worst of the heat is moving east and it should start cooling soon.

items.title: Milei’s Cabinet chief resigns months after private jet scandal
items.description: Resignation marks a sharp reversal for the government after President Milei himself repeatedly brushed off the revelations about Manuel Adorni, deriding them as attacks orchestrated by the opposition and local media. Leer más

items.title: New laws aim to make it harder to launder ill-gotten gains through property
items.description: Anti-money laundering laws mean that for the first time, real estate agents, lawyers, accountants and others must report suspicious transactions.

items.title: West Asia war LIVE: U.S., Iran agree to halt attacks and hold talks in Doha this week, report says
items.description: The two sides anticipate meeting ‌on June 30 ​in Doha, ⁠Axios report said, to work ‌out their dispute over the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: What France gets wrong about air conditioning
items.description: Is this the heatwave that finally changes French minds on air conditioning? Not the mind of environment minister Monique Barbut, for one, who said she was "horrified" by the idea of it becoming widespread. The rest of the world looked on perplexed as France suffered a brutal week, particularly for schools, hospitals and care homes. Long suspicious of it, French people are slowly adopting A/C, but it remains politically controversial and difficult to install in historic towns and cities.

items.title: Watch: A race against the clock for Venezuela earthquake rescuers
items.description: There is a sense that the next few hours will be critical for saving anyone who is still trapped, says the BBC's Dan Johnson reporting from the border of Venezuela and Columbia.

items.title: New Caledonia election tests territory's future after unrest and deadlock
items.description: New Caledonia goes to the poll on Sunday in crucial elections. The possibility of independence from France remains the central political issue, with today’s vote taking place peacefully. The election comes two years after plans to expand voting rights to thousands of non-Indigenous residents triggered deadly unrest and forced the postponement of elections in 2024. Eliza Herbert reports.

items.title: Turkey police detain dozens at LGBTQ+ Pride event in Istanbul
items.description: Turkish police on Sunday detained at least 50 people, including a journalist, during a LGBTQ+ Pride event in Istanbul that went ahead despite a ban by local authorities. Homosexuality is not illegal in Turkey, but since 2015, the annual Pride march has been almost systematically banned and suppressed.

items.title: Comedy legend Mel Brooks turns 100
items.description: A parody of Adolf Hitler in the landmark 1968 movie "The Producers" was just the beginning for US actor Mel Brooks. The 100-year-old comedian is still on a roll today.

items.title: Will Germany impose an age limit for social media?
items.description: A quarter of German youth spend too much time on social media, with 350,000 considered to have an addiction problem. Education Minister Karin Prien is advocating for a legal minimum age of 13.

items.title: Messi scores again as Argentina head into World Cup last 32 on a high
items.description: Skipper came off the bench to score his sixth goal of the tournament as Argentina warmed up for their World Cup knockout tie with Cape Verde by beating Jordan. Leer más

items.title: In Lebanon, framework agreement signed with Israel spurs protest, criticism
items.description: Anger over deal that does not force Israeli troops to withdraw from occupied land after months of deadly attacks.

items.title: Venezuela death toll reaches 1,430
items.description: More than three days after Venezuela was struck by its most powerful earthquake in over a century, hopes of finding survivors are fading. Experts say the first 72 hours are critical for rescues, after which the operation increasingly becomes one of recovering bodies. The confirmed death toll has climbed to 1,430 and continues to rise by the hundreds, while tens of thousands of people remain missing. The UN's migration agency estimates that nearly seven million people were affected by Wednesday's twin earthquakes and are in need of shelter, clean water and other essential aid. Yinka Oyetade reports.

items.title: Live: Work on Victoria's Big Build should halt until 'corruption' dealt with: Hume
items.description: Deputy federal Liberal leader Jane Hume has called for a pause in construction on several major Victorian infrastructure projects until "corruption" is dealt with. Follow live.

items.title: Two boys rescued from Venezuela earthquake rubble after days of being trapped
items.description: Rescuers spent six hours carefully digging to reach one of the boys, as many residents continue searching through the rubble by hand, still waiting for heavy machinery to arrive.

items.title: United States and Iran trade fresh strikes further testing 60-day truce
items.description: Tehran said it launched strikes against US targets in Bahrain and Kuwait in retaliation for an earlier strike launched by Washington on Iran.

items.title: Russell beats Verstappen at Austrian Grand Prix to boost F1 title hopes
items.description: George Russell's victory over Max Verstappen further reduces teammate Kimi Antonelli’s lead in the drivers' title race.

items.title: Civilian plane crash kills 11 in France: local authorities
items.description: The aircraft, registered in Germany, crashed in a grassy area near the runway of the Nancy-Essey aerodrome, close to a residential area and two roads

items.title: The national program teaching a 'hushed topic' to boys and girls
items.description: Educators behind a national program teaching high school students about periods and pelvic pain hope it will encourage empathy.

items.title: LIVE: South Africa 0-1 Canada – Canada into last 16 of FIFA World Cup
items.description: Live updates, analyses and text commentary as South Africa face cohosts Canada in first knockout match in Los Angeles.

items.title: Australian man charged with murder after dead girl found in suitcase in Thailand
items.description: The 17-year-old's body was found discarded near a railway track on Saturday, police say.

items.title: Press freedom under threat in Uganda as military chief orders closures
items.description: Independent media group, NMG, said it was under a "military siege" after Muhoozi Kainerugaba shutdown its newspapers, TV station, and radio outlets.

items.title: Australia and Vanuatu set to sign strategic pact after months of negotiations
items.description: Vanuatu's Prime Minister Jotham Napat and Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese are set to ink the Nakamal Agreement, almost 10 months after Mr Napat pulled out of a planned signing ceremony in Port Vila, citing concerns around sovereignty.

items.title: DGS withdraws restriction on the movement of Indian ships in the Strait of Hormuz
items.description: The advisory against deploying Indian seafarers in the conflict zones also stands withdrawn

items.title: Rome celebrates 80 years of Italy's iconic Vespa scooter
items.description: Thousands of Vespa riders took to Rome's streets to mark Vespa's 80th birthday. The wasp-waisted scooter became a symbol of Italy's rebirth after World War II.

items.title: Why do reports of domestic abuse increase during World Cups?
items.description: Why do reports of domestic abuse increase during World Cups?

items.title: World Cup 2026: Colombia secure top spot ahead of disappointing Portugal
items.description: Portugal needed to beat Colombia to reclaim first place in Group K. But in the end, it was Colombia who came closest to victory. The two teams ultimately drew.

items.title: Venezuela earthquakes: Nearly 7 million may be impacted — UN
items.description: The projection includes up to two million people in the capital, Caracas. Rescue teams are racing against time to save survivors, three days after the twin quakes that killed 1,450 people. DW has more.

items.title: In Venezuela, silence has become a rescue tool
items.description: In Venezuela rescue crews now stop almost everything and ask for silence so they can hear anyone still alive underneath the rubble. NPR's Eyder Peralta reports from the port city of La Guaira.

items.title: Qld government dumps plan to turn gold mine into pumped hydro
items.description: A $6 billion dollar project to rehabilitate a gold mine and turn it into a pumped hydro power station has been dumped by the Queensland government.

items.title: News in Frames | Khukuri: a symbol of courage and loyalty
items.description: Once used by the Gorkha soldiers to fight the British Army, the khukuri knife’s uses have transformed over time according to the needs of the Nepali people, who continue to use it extensively in combat as well as in their homes

items.title: France records around 1,000 excess deaths during heatwave
items.description: The Public Health France agency said Sunday that around 1,000 more deaths than expected occurred during a record-breaking heatwave that baked much of western Europe for days, adding that 85 percent of the casualties were people aged 65 and over. The agency stressed that the figures were preliminary and were likely to be an underestimate.

items.title: Why is Germany taking a big stake in a major tankmaker?
items.description: The German government will buy a 40% stake in tankmaker KNDS. The deal is as much about control in the critical defense sector as it is about the Franco-German relationship.

items.description: The aircraft belonged to state oil giant Aramco and the cause of the crash is being investigated.

items.title: Quick hits: Canada's emotional match-winner and scenes in rural Kansas
items.description: Canada's heroes tread new ground, South Korea's extreme reaction to going out and scenes in Algeria's home away from home. Here's the five quick hits from the World Cup.

items.title: Israel strikes south Lebanon despite framework agreement to end hostilities

items.title: Lawyers under pressure as court backlog hits 'crisis point'
items.description: Routine cases are dragging through Tasmanian courts for years as senior lawyers lament the "crisis" engulfing the state's criminal justice system.

items.title: Iran warns ships not to bypass its chosen Hormuz route
items.description: At the moment, Iran insists ships transiting the strait pass through a corridor close to its own shores, though this week dozens of vessels have travelled along the opposite side of the waterway, hugging the Omani coast

items.title: Israel strikes Lebanon, testing days-old peace deal
items.description: Hezbollah calls the deal a surrender as Israeli forces stay put and continue striking the south.

items.title: New Caledonia heads to polls in vote delayed since 2024
items.description: Security has been high at polling stations as the South Pacific archipelago holds its first provincial elections since 2019. The vote in the French territory was originally set for 2024 but was delayed by violent unrest.

items.title: Israel army says killed 'armed terrorists' in southern Syria
items.description: The Israeli army has occupied what it calls a "security zone" in southern Syria since late 2024 and said it would remain in place there "in order to remove any threat to Israeli civilians and IDF soldiers"

items.title: Tehran targets Bahrain and Kuwait after US attacks
items.description: The ceasefire between Iran and the United States appears increasingly fragile on Sunday, following a new exchange of strikes. In the latest escalation, Tehran launched missiles and drones at Bahrain and Kuwait, both of which host US military bases, just hours after the United States struck multiple targets across Iran. The renewed violence comes amid rising tensions over efforts to fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a key global shipping route.

items.title: Drone captures ongoing rescue efforts after Venezuela earthquakes
items.description: Drone footage from Venezuela's La Guaira shows widespread destruction after twin earthquakes devastated the region.

items.title: Heatwave spreads across eastern Europe, France records 1,000 deaths in a week
items.description: The record-breaking heatwave in France is set to come to an end on Sunday, with the country's extreme weather alert expected to be lifted by the evening, bringing relief after temperatures climbed as high as 40°C this week. But the heatwave is far from over, as it shifts eastward, with Germany and the Czech Republic now preparing for the worst of the extreme weather.

items.title: Why Kim Jong Un never talks about his mother
items.description: Very few North Koreans know about her, as her origin could threaten the regime’s legitimacy.

items.title: Budapest's first Pride since Orban left power
items.description: Budapest held its first Pride march since the end of former prime minister Viktor Orban's 16-year government with thousands turning out to celebrate.

items.title: 6.1 magnitude earthquake off northeastern coastline rattles Japan; no tsunami warning issued
items.description: No tsunami warning was issued, and there were no immediate reports of damage or casualties. But the recent spate of quakes coming during typhoon season has set off worries about mudslides

items.title: Hope of finding more Venezuela quake survivors fades despite late rescues
items.description: Those rescued include 60-year-old woman trapped for 86 hours, as tens of thousands still reported missing.

items.title: Reporter's Notebook: What its like to report from an Ebola outbreak

items.title: Argentina: Milei's top aide resigns over corruption scandal
items.description: Manuel Adorni is mired in a corruption scandal but has denied wrongdoing. Javier Milei has defended Adorni, saying, "Manuel is innocent."

items.title: Serbia’s Vučić to resign, opening path to early elections amid political tensions
items.description: Serbia’s President Aleksandar Vučić has announced that he will resign within weeks, paving the way for early elections. There is speculation that the populist leader could attempt to move into the more powerful role of Prime Minister. The announcement comes after more than a year of heightened tensions in the Balkan nation, where university students have led protests demanding political change.

The election comes two years after plans to expand voting rights to thousands of non-Indigenous residents triggered deadly unrest and forced the postponement of elections in 2024. items.title: World Cup 2026 : 215 goals in record breaking group stage
items.description: Austria and Algeria went down to the wire in a match full of drama. Austria's last-minute goal means both teams are through to the next round. Celebrations are also due for the Democratic Republic of Congo who have qualified for the first time in their history, thanks to their 3-1 win against Uzbekistan. Portugal and Colombia stayed level at 0-0. England finished top of group L, putting two past Panama. The round of 32 fixtures are all confirmed, the first knockout game sees Canada go up against South Africa in Los Angeles.

The victory means nine of the tournament's ten African teams have advanced to the next round, joining South Africa, Morocco, Senegal, Ivory Coast, Cape Verde, Egypt, Algeria and Ghana. For some teams, including Congo, it marks the first time they have reached this stage of the competition.

items.title: Armed conflict threatens Ethiopia’s ancient monasteries
items.description: Lake Tana in Ethiopia’s Amhara region was once a tourist hotspot, known for its picturesque shorelines and monasteries dating back to the 14th century. But since 2023, an ongoing war between the Fano militias and federal government forces has scared away the tourists. Cities are subject to shootings and grenade attacks, while rural areas face drone strikes and kidnappings for ransom. Now, the future of these ancient monasteries is uncertain. This report by our correspondent Tom Canetti.

items.title: Jesica Cirio's dollar videos rock case against ex-Buenos Aires Province official Martín Insaurralde
items.description: Images of the model displaying stacks of cash in the home she once shared with veteran Kirchnerite politician Martín Insaurralde have put the courts’ corruption file back on centre stage. Authorities seek to determine whether the former official’s assets can be explained by his declared income. Leer más

items.title: Death toll from double earthquake strike in Venezuela rises to 188
items.description: Venezuela mounts desperate search for survivors after twin quakes kill at least 188 and injure hundreds; Offers of rescue support, aid and condolences pour in. Leer más

items.title: Messi celebrates 39th birthday at the top of the world
items.description: Argentina's all-time World Cup top scorer has been in unstoppable form, netting all five of his side's goals in two opening victories. As he celebrates his 39th birthday, are we finally coming to the end of the star's career? Leer más

items.title: Two things this week gave clues about the global future
items.description: Two things happened last week that give us a much clearer picture of what the world is going to be like from now on.

items.title: Manuel Adorni finally resigns as Cabinet chief after months of scandal
items.description: Cabinet chief finally steps down after months of pressure, citing relentless media attacks; Official admitted he concealed US$500,000 from his asset declarations. Resignation is a significant blow to Javier Milei's inner circle. Leer más

items.title: West Asia war highlights: U.S. conducts further strikes against 'multiple targets' in Iran
items.description: Lebanon's President hopes U.S. will press Israel to withdraw from southern areas

items.title: Uzbekistan makes its World Cup debut, a first for Central Asia
items.description: The country is the first Central Asian nation to qualify for the World Cup, and Uzbek fans have reveled in showcasing their country and culture. The country's president calls the team a symbol of the "new Uzbekistan."

items.title: 'If you are alive, make any noise': Venezuela searches rubble on day 4
items.description: Rescue teams are racing against time after twin earthquakes killed more than 1,400 people and left tens of thousands unaccounted for.

items.title: German lawyers: Ban on far-right AfD 'likely successful'
items.description: A new report by legal experts has found that efforts to ban Germany's far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) could be successful — but there would be political dangers to such a move.

items.title: Albania's "Flamingo revolution": Trump-linked luxury resort plan triggers mass protests
items.description: Protests in Albania entered their fifth week on Saturday, with demonstrations that began in May over a planned luxury resort project linked to the Trump family. Since then, the movement has expanded into a broader criticism of Prime Minister Edi Rama and his government, with protesters raising wider concerns about governance and political accountability. Vladimir Karaj, a journalist and editor at the Balkan Investigative Reporting Network, joins us from Tirana for further insights.

items.title: Messi rules the World Cup – but are Argentina too reliant on him?
items.description: Five goals in two games have confirmed the Argentine captain as the tournament's all-time leading scorer. But with Lautaro Martínez and Julián Álvarez yet to find the net, questions are growing about what happens if and when Messi's legs give out. Leer más

items.title: More than 1,300 excess deaths recorded in Europe heatwave: WHO
items.description: At least 191 million people are forecast to endure temperatures of at least 35℃ on June 28, in Europe, with the heat particularly intense in Germany, the Czech Republic, Hungary and Poland, according to AFP estimates

items.title: Frozen feasts and cool pools help Rome zoo animals survive heatwave
items.description: As a severe heatwave grips Italy, Rome's Bioparco is helping its animals stay cool with frozen treats, chilled pools and climate-controlled shelters while the capital remains under a red heat alert.

items.title: Burkina Faso cuts diplomatic ties with France
items.description: Burkina Faso has cut diplomatic ties with France, marking a dramatic break with its former colonial ruler. The military junta led by Captain Ibrahim Traoré accused Paris of repeatedly acting against the country's interests, deepening its shift away from Western allies.

items.title: Telstra phasing out CAN Radio phone system amid customer safety concerns
items.description: Telstra's Customer Access Network Radio has been a lifeline for many for more than 40 years but its technology has reached the end of its life. Customers now fear a new system will not be as reliable in an emergency.

items.title: Ukraine: Zaporizhzhia ablaze after Russian missile and drone attack leaves at least nine hurt
items.description: Russian missile and drone strikes set an office building ablaze in central Zaporizhzhia on 26 June, injuring at least nine people and triggering a major emergency response.

items.title: A historian analyzes whether Ukraine has a localized advantage in modern combat power
items.description: NPR's Scott Simon talks with strategic studies professor Phillips O'Brien of St Andrew's University in Scotland about whether Ukraine has gained the upper hand in the fight against Russia.

items.title: Trips, wealth and property – The judicial cases closing in on Manuel Adorni
items.description: Investigators have ordered a forensic review of the former Cabinet chief's finances and are examining the circumstances of his private jet trip to Uruguay and real-estate investments. Leer más

items.title: Ben Stokes, England test captain, to retire from international cricket
items.description: England test captain Ben Stokes will retire from international cricket after the ongoing test match against New Zealand.

items.title: Banning social media for teens: Solution or overreaction?
items.description: One in seven teens in the European Union spends more than eight hours a day in front of screens, a new study shows. Are social media bans the answer?

items.title: Ebola cases jump as Congo tightens travel rules
items.description: Health officials said increased epidemiological surviellance was helping to detect cases earlier. They also said that community spread of the virus had grown

items.title: Rangnick dismisses collusion talk after Austria and Algeria advance after ‘mad’ World Cup draw
items.description: Austria coach Ralf Rangnick dismissed suggestions his team had played for a draw against Algeria after Saturday's 3-3 World Cup match sent both teams through to the knockout stage. Austria will now face off against Spain in the round of 32 on July 2 in California.

items.title: Bayer: Why so many Roundup weedkiller lawsuits?
items.description: Bayer was sued for failing to add cancer warnings to its controversial pesticide Roundup when US regulators opposed adding one. DW asks if the latest Supreme Court win will shield the chemical giant from paying billions.

items.title: Magdeburg Christmas market attacker sentenced to life in prison
items.description: A Magdeburg court has sentenced a 51-year-old to life over his 2024 attack on a Christmas market in the city. The trained psychiatrist drove an SUV through a barricade and into a crowd, killing six and injuring hundreds.

items.title: Algorithms, Artificial Intelligence and Milei’s ethics
items.description: Milei has a militant and narrow world view that he’s tied to a vision that integrates AI and tech billionaires. Those same players have a global agenda that they will pursue, and Argentina will only be part of that plan for as long as it is useful for them. Leer más

items.title: Australia to double maximum penalty for platforms in breach of social media ban
items.description: Children under the age of 16 have been prevented from 10 key social media platforms in Australia since December 2025.

items.title: Germany's development aid strategy under fire
items.description: Days ahead of the launch of the North-South Commission, development aid organizations have urged the German government to reverse billions in cuts to development aid.

items.title: Military-age Ukrainian men could lose EU protection
items.description: The European Commission has suggested withdrawing protection status for military-age Ukrainian males. A number of EU countries support the move — and so does Kyiv.

items.title: New Caledonia votes: High stakes in France's Pacific territory
items.description: New Caledonia voted Sunday in local elections that are key to determining the French Pacific territory’s political future and relationship with France. FRANCE 24’s Leela Jacinto explains the stakes and how decolonization and demographics play critical roles in France’s Pacific foothold.

items.title: Milei’s ‘Super RIGI’ investment bill approved by lower house
items.description: La Libertad Avanza’s beefed-up version of RIGI investment scheme offers improved tax breaks and benefits for investors splashing US$1-billion into hi-tech developing industries ; Bill now heads to Senate. Leer más

items.title: Skydiving plane crashes in France, killing all 11 people on board
items.description: A skydiving plane crashed in eastern France on Sunday, killing all 11 people on board. The crash in the town of Tomblaine killed five instructors, five students and the pilot, according to local officials. The cause of the incident was not immediately clear, and a technical investigation has been opened, according to a French deputy public prosecutor.

items.title: US seizes nearly 400 websites that were illegally streaming World Cup, DOJ says
Key figure in South African police corruption scandal pleads guilty
items.description: Controversial businessman Vusimusi “Cat” Matlala admitted to fraud, corruption, and money laundering linked to a police service tender.

items.title: Fact check: As Europe bakes, should you trust your sunscreen?
items.description: As a heat wave sweeps across Europe, claims are resurfacing online questioning whether sunscreen is safe or linked to skin cancer risk.

items.title: Germany considers 'opt-out' organ donor option
items.description: Germany is one of few European countries that still has an exclusively "opt-in" choice for organ donation — despite long waiting lists for organs. Now, parliament is debating a change.

items.title: Venezuela races to find survivors
items.description: The search for survivors is entering a critical stage in Venezuela. NPR'S Eyder Peralta reports from La Guaira where rescue crews are still hoping to find people alive.

items.title: With or without Messi? Argentina's big question against Jordan
items.description: Saturday's rotated side could still include players eager to maintain rhythm and those seeking it. Julián Álvarez is expected to start, with changes to the defence and midfield also likely. Leer más

items.title: Corruption, our daily bread
items.description: An unrelenting cascade of corruption scandals enjoys political and judicial protection, while the government negotiates key appointments to put the brakes on cases at Comodoro Py. Leer más

items.title: Saudi Aramco helicopter crash kills 14 nationals, state news agency says
items.description: “Investigations are under ​way, with the participation of relevant authorities, ⁠to determine the causes of the helicopter crash,” ‌the state news agency added

items.title: Three firefighters killed in US wildfires along Colorado–Utah border
items.description: The US has seen a harsh wildfire season this year, with prolonged droughts and dry conditions fuelling blazes.

items.title: Germany: Racism is almost a daily reality for Muslims
items.description: Insults, discrimination, violence, deaths: Experts warn of growing anti-Muslim racism in Germany — and criticize policymakers.

items.description: A selection of the stories that caught our eye over the last seven days in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: With 'Operation Purgatory,' Magyar moves to demolish Orban system
items.description: The new Hungarian government is moving swiftly to dismantle the system of former Prime Minister Viktor Orban. The focus: fighting corruption, reforming the media and protecting democracy.

items.title: Iran attacks Bahrain, Kuwait following U.S. strikes, threatens to end talks to end war
items.description: An interim deal between the U.S. and Iran to end the war called for the Strait, which once saw a fifth of the world's oil and natural gas pass through it, to see transits resume

items.title: France: 11 killed in civilian plane crash
items.description: Police have urged people to "strictly avoid" the area around the airport in Tomblaine. The plane was reportedly carrying skydivers.

items.title: Race against the clock in Venezuela to find survivors of twin quakes
items.description: Nearly 69,000 people have been reported missing following the disaster that left at least 3,200 people injured and thousands homeless.

items.description: Buildings collapse in capital Caracas; Delcy Rodriguez says 20 aftershocks followed the earthquakes

items.title: Ukraine strikes Russian weapons plant, Moscow fuel hub
items.description: The two attacks, in Volgograd and Vladimir regions, are part of President Zelenskyy's strategy to bring the war to the Russian people and force Putin into peace negotiations.

items.title: Rwanda's royal cattle pampered with poetry and music
items.description: The Inyambo breed at the King's Palace Museum have become living symbols of the nation's cultural heritage.

items.title: Inside a secretive Ukrainian team launching deep drone strikes at Russia
items.description: Ukraine's long-range drones are striking deep inside Russia, up to 1,200 miles away, hitting oil refineries and depots. NPR recently spent time with one of the Ukrainian strike teams launching drones at Russian targets.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Defence seeks acquittal for former naval commander in ARA San Juan submarine trial
items.description: Prosecutors on Monday urged a five-year prison sentence for Claudio Villamide, commander of the submarine force at the time of the tragedy; Defence lawyers request acquittal, arguing there is no evidence linking his actions to the deaths of the crew Leer más

items.title: Togo reforms unconstitutional, rules ECOWAS court
items.description: Togo’s constitutional reform establishing a parliamentary system was unconstitutional, according to the ECOWAS Court of Justice.

items.title: The unsurprising fall of Sir Keir Starmer
items.description: From the word go, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer behaved as though he enjoyed the support of most of his country’s population, an illusion that was encouraged by the worshipful treatment he received from “progressives” in the media. Leer más

items.title: Tunisian rights activist sentenced to 25 years in prison
items.description: The IVD, set up after the 2011 revolution ousted longtime ruler Zine El Abidine Ben Ali, heard testimony from thousands of his victims and those of his predecessor Habib Bourguiba.

items.title: World Cup: 'Pride Match' sparks objections in Egypt and Iran
items.description: Friday night's game in Seattle was designated as a "Pride Match" well ahead of the World Cup draw. The fixture list then threw up Egypt vs. Iran, two countries in which homosexuality is criminalized.

items.title: New Caledonia polls close in French territory's first provincial elections since 2019
items.description: Voting in the French overseas territory of New Caledonia closed on Sunday, with a turnout of 54.42 per cent, as citizens participated in the archipelago's first provincial elections since 2019. The results of the election, initially planned for 2024, will determine the balance of power in New Caledonia ahead of fresh negotiations with France on the territory's status, with independence remaining the defining political issue.

items.title: Fact check: Throwing cold water on heat wave disinformation
items.description: An outdated air-conditioning law, a viral health myth about sleeping with the fan on and the age-old climate change hoax allegation: DW checked what's spreading in Europe's heat wave.

items.title: Milei shuns Wall Street to raise dollars at rate lower than 7%
items.description: Luis Caputo stuck to a strategy that many investors initially saw as too risky: secure dollars through cheaper alternative channels, buy time and wait for spreads to come down. Leer más

items.title: As drug use increases in Burundi, a centre helps addicts recover
items.description: Strong Bridge centre in Bujumbura has helped hundreds of young people recover since it opened its doors in 2021.

items.title: Argentines in arrears: 1 in every 4 families cannot pay their debts
items.description: In the past year, 2.5 million debtors have fallen behind with payments. Some 3.3 million people currently owe payments dating back more than a year for sums averaging approximately 1.6 million pesos. Private studies show the situation facing families in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Ukrainian strike ignites Russian oil refinery, as Putin acknowledges 'difficult period'
items.description: Debris from downed Ukrainian drones sparked a blaze at a major oil refinery in Russia on Sunday, according to local authorities. This comes as Russian President Vladimir Putin said that his country was “going through a difficult period”. In recent months, Ukraine has stepped up its long-range attacks on Russian military facilities, choking Moscow's fuel supplies and military deliveries.

items.title: Baby, young boys pulled alive from Venezuela earthquake rubble
items.description: The three children are among at least 33 people rescued during a weekend multinational operation that continued searching for survivors, while thousands remained unaccounted-for.

items.title: US allows partial release of Anthropic's Mythos AI model
items.description: Only a small group of American firms will get access to Anthropic's AI Model Mythos 5, however, it is unclear which companies will be selected.

Indian firm, CEO among entities sanctioned by U.S. for ‘fuelling’ civil war in Sudan
items.description: The U.S. Treasury Department’s Office of Foreign Assets Control said the targeted networks enabled both the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces to expand and intensify civil war

items.title: Sort Your Life Out: The four most common cluttering mistakes and how to fix them
items.description: Tidying expert Dilly Carter shares her best tips on organising your kitchen, lounge and clothes.

items.description: Hezbollah leader Qassem called on the government to withdraw from "its sins that are ruining Lebanon".

items.title: War on Iran | The story of a shipwrecked hegemon
items.description: The war the U.S. and Israel launched has come to a fragile pause with a preliminary agreement between Washington and Tehran, but it has already diminished America’s presence in the region, prompting Gulf countries to seek a new modus vivendi with Tehran, while Israel, isolated and angry, is clinging on to its Lebanon card

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: Human rights expert Philippe Sands wins German Peace Prize
items.description: From advocating for the law of ecocide to representing Palestinian rights, the descendant of Holocaust survivors tirelessly fights against genocide and crimes against humanity.

items.title: US close to allowing Anthropic to restore Fable 5 model, Axios reports
Venezuela declares state of emergency after powerful twin earthquakes
items.description: Two massive earthquakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening; It remains unknown whether the incident caused fatalities, but some buildings collapsed and residents were driven into the streets in panic. Leer más

items.title: Solidarity rally in Ohio after court ends protected status for Haitians
items.description: Residents gathered Thursday in Ohio in support of Haitian migrants following a Supreme Court decision to allow the Trump administration to end legal protections for migrants from Haiti and Syria fleeing violence and natural disaster in their country

items.title: South Africa's President Ramaphosa warns anti-immigrant groups over June 30 'deadline'
items.description: South Africa's president has warned anti-immigration groups against trying to impose the so-called June 30th deadline for illegal migrants to leave the country.

items.title: Royal commission spotlight falls on 'unregulated cesspool of disinformation'
items.description: The "cesspool" of anti-Jewish material on social media is set to be examined when the antisemitism royal commission resumes this week.

items.title: Key poll in New Caledonia: First major vote on French Pacific islands since 2024 riots
items.description: Voting in the French overseas territory of New Caledonia closed on Sunday, with a turnout of 54.42 per cent, as citizens participated in the archipelago's first provincial elections since 2019. The results of the election, initially planned for 2024, will determine the balance of power in New Caledonia ahead of fresh negotiations with France on the territory's status, with independence remaining the defining political issue. Political scientist and post-colonial activist, Françoise Vergès, joins us for further insights.

items.title: Starmer couldn’t master the media. Can Burnham?
items.description: Keir Starmer’s rapid fall from power and Britain’s revolving door of leaders.

items.title: World Cup: Morocco's Ismael Saibari set for Bayern Munich
items.description: Three nationalities, five languages and soon to be resident in a fourth country: Ismael Saibari, the star of Morocco's World Cup so far, is set to join Bayern Munich. But he has business to finish with the Atlas Lions.

items.title: Hungary holds first post-Orban Budapest Pride march
items.description: Tens of thousands packed Budapest's streets despite scorching heat. Former right-wing leader Viktor Orban tried to ban the march last year as part of a wider LGBTQ+ crackdown.

items.title: Israeli forces bulldoze olive groves in the occupied West Bank
items.description: Israeli forces bulldozed olive trees in the village of Zububa, west of Jenin in the occupied West Bank.

items.title: Pakistan's Geo News apologizes for Muharram broadcast
items.description: The broadcaster Geo News was handed a 15-day suspension for a documentary that was "liable to hurt the religious sentiments of viewers," Pakistan's media watchdog said.

items.title: Man charged with murder after woman shot on the Gold Coast
items.description: A 23-year-old man is due to appear in court, charged with murder, after he allegedly shot and killed a woman on the Gold Coast.

items.description: Ivory Coast reaches World Cup knockout stage for the first time after Nicolas Pépé’s brace beats Curaçao 2-0, sparking huge celebrations in Abidjan.

items.title: Previously unseen archives reveal history of Australian merino wool
items.description: More than 1,000 kilograms of wool samples, lab notes and technical drawings spent decades gathering dust in a British home. A Victorian sheep farmer just brought them back.

items.description: Rescue teams worked through the ruins of collapsed buildings in La Guaira on 25 June after twin earthquakes devastated northern Venezuela, killing at least 188 people and injuring around 1,500.

items.description: Japan reach World Cup last 32 after 1-1 draw with Sweden on 26 June, prompting celebrations across Tokyo. , sparking huge celebrations in Tokyo’s Shibuya. They now face Brazil in the knockout stage.

items.title: Why Germany's Deutsche Bahn will face delays for many years
items.description: Delayed, unreliable and forced into long detours due to construction: Germany no longer sets the pace when it comes to rail travel — and this is unlikely to change anytime soon. The company is mired in a deep crisis.

items.description: The exhibition runs through July 19, with the world's oldest known football on display from June 22 to 27—offering visitors a rare glimpse into the origins of a game that continues to unite billions around the world.

items.description: An estimated 150 million people have been experiencing temperatures of over 35C across Europe.

items.description: An alarming number of young people are turning away from the news, according to the Reuters Digital News Report presented at DW's Global Media Forum. Despite this, the report's author says, "journalism still matters."

Artyom Avetisyan, a cardiothoracic surgery registrar at the Royal Hobart Hospital, has been remembered by hospital staff as a kind and compassionate colleague who will be "greatly missed".

items.title: Russia's Putin promises security as Ukraine ramps up attacks
items.description: The Russian leader condemned what he called "terrorist attacks on our territory and infrastructure facilities." Ukraine has targeted oil refineries and weapons factories, causing massive disruption in recent weeks.

items.title: New US-Iran exchange of attacks threatens ceasefire

Efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz without Iran's direct oversight sparked the crossfire now gripping the region. France 24 correspondent Reza Sayah tells the latest from Tehran.

items.description: Germany had already qualified for the knockouts of the 2026 World Cup, but their performance was still disappointing in a 2-1 defeat.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Uganda army chief shuts down leading independent media group
items.description: Muhoozi Kainerugaba, the head of Uganda's military and President Yoweri Museveni's son, has asserted growing control in recent months, ordering the arrests of politicians, activists and now a media shutdown

items.title: Six terrorists killed, one captured after Rangers’ base attack in Pakistan’s Karachi
items.description: Four soldiers dead; attack claimed by Jamaat-ul-Ahrar, a militant faction of the TTP that has largely operated in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province bordering Afghanistan

items.title: Millie Bobby Brown and Louis Partridge on belly laughs, sibling vibes and Enola Holmes
items.description: Brown talks to the BBC about having her "hands and heart" all over the detective adventure series.

items.title: King Charles won't live in Buckingham Palace, reveals taxes
items.description: Buckingham Palace has been the British monarch's official residence in London for nearly two centuries. But royal officials say King Charles and Queen Camilla won't reside there.

items.title: World Cup: Ivory Coast into knockouts for 1st time with win over Curaçao
items.description: Les Éléphants have crashed the party and made it out of their group for the first time in four World Cup appearances. Pépé scored in each half and Ivory Coast advanced with a 2-0 victory over Curaçao on Thursday

items.title: Panic attacks and fractures
items.description: The BBC travelled to a Caracas hospital that is treating people from the worst affected areas.

items.title: Timbuktu without water or electricity as fuel shortage shuts down power station
items.description: Timbuktu has been without water or electricity for four days, according to a report by RFI. A monthlong fuel shortage has brought the Malian city’s thermal power station to a halt - shutting down basic services.

items.title: SpaceX, Charter discuss mobile phone partnership in US, Bloomberg News says
Newborn baby rescued from rubble of Venezuela quake
items.description: Video shared on social media shows rescuers bringing the infant out to applause in the hardest-hit city of La Guaira, north of the capital Caracas. Leer más

items.title: Exports boom but households struggle as INDEC posts 2.3% growth
items.description: GDP rose 2.3% in the first quarter of 2026 year-on-year, driven by exports and extractive industries, but unemployment is up, bank defaults have hit a 20-year high and manufacturing is contracting. Leer más

items.title: Supreme Court orders Milei government to fund universities
items.description: Ruling upholds university financing law vetoed by the President and ends a two-year legal battle over funding, salaries and student support. Leer más

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: Lebanon: thousands mark Ashoura in Nabatieh amid rubble left by Israeli strikes
items.description: Thousands of Shiite Muslims marked Ashoura in Nabatieh on 26 June, holding processions through neighbourhoods devastated by recent Israeli airstrikes in a powerful display of resilience.

items.title: France records around 1,000 additional deaths amid extreme heat wave leading to European records
items.description: France has experienced a surge in deaths during its record-breaking heat wave, according to the country’s public health agency

items.title: Baidu's AI chip unit Kunlunxin targets $50 billion Hong Kong IPO, The Information reports
Europe heatwave made possible by climate change, scientists say
items.description: Scientists say the latest findings underscore the urgency of both reducing emissions and adapting infrastructure to withstand more frequent and intense heatwaves in the years ahead.

items.title: Cape Verde makes history, Iran left waiting & Senegal shatters records
items.description: Cape Verde is through to the World Cup knockout stage in its tournament debut. A 0-0 draw with Saudi Arabia sealed an incredible run after holding Spain national football team and Uruguay national football team earlier in the group.

items.title: Messi in Dallas, messy down here
items.description: The separation of powers is alive and well in Argentina when it comes to corruption – regardless of whether the issue is Cabinet Chief Manuel Adorni’s assets or the up to US$20 million stacked in the San Vicente mansion, the knee-jerk response of the political world is: “The matter belongs to the courts.” Leer más

items.title: Creative climate messaging and other eco wins of the week
items.description: An artist in Mali and climate scientists in the US are finding new ways to inform the public about the climate crisis.
